meta.topic: Using present continuous and present simple

step_1_context.text: We often use the present simple and present continuous when telling stories and jokes in informal spoken English to create the impression that events are happening now. This can make them more direct

step_2_explanation.simple_rule: We often use the present simple and present continuous when telling stories and jokes in informal spoken English to create the impression that events are happening now.
step_2_explanation.formula:
We also use the present simple and present continuous in live commentaries (for example, on sports events) when the report takes place at the same time as the action:
 • King serves to the left-hand court and Adams makes a wonderful return.
step_2_explanation.common_mistake: We can use the present simple in phrases such as It says here, I hear, I gather, I see, I understand and They say, (Someone) says, (Someone) tells me to introduce news that we have heard, read, seen (e.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 2.1.1 Rodriguez ___ to De Mello, who ___ just over the bar. The home team ___ much more in this half...
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Live commentary: use present simple for quick actions.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 2.1.2 A man ___ home late one night after the office Christmas party. His wife ___ for him, and she ___ to him...
options: is arriving / is waiting / is saying
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Story: simple for main event, continuous for background.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 2.1.3 I went to a concert yesterday in the Town Hall. In the middle of it, while the orchestra ___, this man suddenly ___ on his seat and ___ to conduct them.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Narratives often use present simple to highlight sudden past events.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 2.2.1 'Government gives health service billions' -> I ___ the government's giving the health service a lot more money.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Introduce news using phrases like 'I see'.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 2.2.2 'Vegecorp to sack 1,000 workers.' -> ___ Vegecorp are going to sack 1,000 workers.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Introduce news using phrases like 'I hear'.
